title: Unchecked 32-bit frame length drives allocation and truncated output framing

direct_impact.operation: attacker-controlled 32-bit frame length drives arena allocation/read attempt; oversized output length is narrowed back to uint32_t
direct_impact.bytes: up to 4294967295 requested input bytes; output framing truncates any serialized response above 4 GiB

chain_potential.standalone: denial of service against local conformance automation and protocol desynchronization if a response ever exceeds 4 GiB
chain_potential.with_other_bugs: limited chaining value because the confirmed behavior is allocation pressure / incorrect framing rather than memory corruption

justification.AV: L - The validated sink is the test-only conformance_upb executable reading attacker-controlled framed stdin, not a network-exposed production library API.
justification.AC: L - The attacker only needs to supply an oversized 32-bit length prefix; no race, heap grooming, or special environment is required.
justification.PR: N - No privileges are required to feed input to the worker process.
justification.UI: N - The vulnerable path executes during normal request processing.
justification.S: U - Impact stays within the conformance worker / automation process.
justification.C: N - No memory disclosure or data exposure was demonstrated.
justification.I: N - The framing truncation effect was demonstrated as a logic flaw, but no real integrity compromise against protected data was validated.
justification.A: L - The confirmed consequence is aborting or desynchronizing a local conformance run, not compromising the broader system.

notes: Reachability is limited to local/test tooling because DoTestIo() is in conformance_upb, not the shipped libprotobuf.a/libupb.a APIs.
Scoring stays availability-only because no memory corruption or information disclosure was reproduced.
